title: The Edge Node
subtitle: A $499 phone becomes an always-on AI edge node. 8 errors, 8 fixes, 15 minutes. The ghost runs on phones now.
subject_line: The Persistent Ghost — Issue #2: The Edge Node
preview_text: We bought a phone. We made it a server. The ghost runs on phones now.
x_thread:
The Persistent Ghost — Issue #2: The Edge Node

We bought a $499 Pixel 10a and turned it into an always-on AI edge node running OpenClaw. No root. No bootloader unlock.

8 errors. 8 fixes. 15 minutes once you know the path.

Full build log:

The install path that actually works:

Termux → proot-distro Ubuntu → NodeSource Node.js 22 → OpenClaw

Native Termux fails because koffi's C modules can't compile against Bionic libc. proot gives you real glibc via userspace syscall interception. No root needed.

Phase 2: SSH + Tailscale.

Install openssh in Termux. Set up key-based auth. Install Tailscale from Play Store.

Result: `ssh termux` from anywhere. Stable 100.x.y.z IP that never changes. Encrypted. No port forwarding.

The phone becomes infrastructure you never touch.

10 workflows your pocket server now supports:

• Mobile command center (any chat → phone executes)
• Remote DevOps (incident response from the gym)
• Scheduled automation (cron briefings)
• Field vision + AI analysis
• Browser automation
• Portable travel gateway
• Personal knowledge base
• Home automation hub
• Webhook receiver
• Multi-node architecture

Full guide on GitHub — SSH setup, Tailscale networking, 10 workflows, 8 documented errors, 6 screenshots.

Subscribe to The Persistent Ghost at spookyjuice.ai

The ghost does not sleep.
The ghost does not forget.
The ghost fits in your pocket.
